quick_facts.label: Award and duration
quick_facts.value: M.Sc. Nursing is a university postgraduate nursing qualification with a prescribed duration of 2 years (3,440 instructional hours in the INC syllabus framework).

quick_facts.label: Baseline eligibility
quick_facts.value: INC eligibility criteria specify registration as a nurse and midwife (or equivalent) with a State Nursing Registration Council; B.Sc. Nursing, B.Sc. (Hons.) Nursing, or Post Basic B.Sc. Nursing with at least 55% aggregate marks; and one year of relevant experience after Basic B.Sc. Nursing or before/after Post Basic B.Sc. Nursing.

quick_facts.label: Core study pattern
quick_facts.value: The INC syllabus includes Nursing Education, Advanced Nursing Practice, Nursing Research and Statistics, Nursing Management, clinical specialty study, clinical practice, and a dissertation.

quick_facts.label: Specialty areas
quick_facts.value: The INC curriculum identifies Medical-Surgical, Obstetric and Gynaecological, Child Health/Paediatric, Mental Health/Psychiatric, and Community Health Nursing; Medical-Surgical study can further cover areas such as critical care, oncology, neurosciences, nephro-urology, orthopaedics, and gastroenterology.

quick_facts.label: Admissions model
quick_facts.value: INC regulations provide for selection on merit in an entrance examination held by the university or another competent authority. Individual institutions publish their own current notices and procedures.

sections.heading: Who can apply
paragraphs: Under the Indian Nursing Council (INC) framework, applicants need to be registered as a nurse and midwife, or equivalent, with a State Nursing Registration Council. They must have completed B.Sc. Nursing, B.Sc. (Hons.) Nursing, or Post Basic B.Sc. Nursing from an INC-recognized institution with at least 55% aggregate marks. The framework also calls for one year of work experience after Basic B.Sc. Nursing, or one year before or after Post Basic B.Sc. Nursing; it states that a 5% marks relaxation for SC/ST candidates may be given.

Applicants should treat these as national baseline criteria and check the particular university’s current prospectus for its specialty-wise requirements, reservation rules, document-verification rules, and any additional conditions.
bullets: Registration: Registered Nurse and Registered Midwife, or equivalent, with a State Nursing Registration Council.
Qualifying degree: B.Sc. Nursing, B.Sc. (Hons.) Nursing, or Post Basic B.Sc. Nursing from an INC-recognized institution.
Minimum marks: 55% aggregate under the INC framework; the syllabus permits a possible 5% relaxation for SC/ST candidates.
Experience: one year after Basic B.Sc. Nursing, or one year before or after Post Basic B.Sc. Nursing.

sections.heading: Duration and workload
paragraphs: The INC M.Sc. Nursing syllabus prescribes a two-year course. Its course-instruction table provides 43 available teaching weeks per year at 40 hours per week, totalling 1,720 hours per year and 3,440 hours over two years. The precise academic calendar, holidays, clinical posting schedule, and examination dates remain university-specific.
bullets: Nominal duration: 2 years.
Indicative INC instructional total: 3,440 hours across two years.
The syllabus includes theory and practical/clinical components in both years.

sections.heading: What students study
paragraphs: The prescribed framework combines advanced professional preparation with specialty practice, education, management, and research. In year one, the scheme lists Nursing Education, Advanced Nursing Practice, Nursing Research and Statistics, and Clinical Specialty I. Year two includes Nursing Management, Clinical Specialty II, and a research dissertation, alongside clinical practice and an educational visit.

Specialty choices in the INC syllabus include Medical-Surgical Nursing, Obstetric and Gynaecological Nursing, Child Health (Paediatric) Nursing, Mental Health (Psychiatric) Nursing, and Community Health Nursing. Medical-Surgical Nursing has listed advanced streams including cardiovascular and thoracic, critical care, oncology, neurosciences, nephro-urology, orthopaedic, and gastroenterology nursing.
bullets: Professional foundations: Nursing Education, Advanced Nursing Practice, Nursing Management, and research/statistics.
Specialty and clinical training: Clinical Specialty I and II with substantial practical hours.
Research: dissertation proposal and final dissertation within the two-year timeline.
Assessment/attendance: the INC framework specifies 75% attendance for theory and practicals and 100% practical attendance before award of the degree.

sections.heading: How admission commonly works
paragraphs: INC regulations state that candidate selection should be based on merit in an entrance examination conducted by the university or competent authority. The test, counselling, seat-allocation process, cut-offs, application dates, and documents are therefore institution-specific rather than set through one single national M.Sc. Nursing entrance route.

For dated institutional context, the AIIMS M.Sc. Nursing 2025 prospectus used an entrance examination for admission: a 90-minute paper of 90 multiple-choice questions, with admission based entirely on entrance performance. This is an example of one institution’s 2025 process, not a universal rule or a current deadline.
bullets: Start with the latest prospectus of the university or institute offering the chosen specialty.
Prepare registration evidence, qualifying-degree records, mark sheets, experience certificates, and recognition-related documents where requested.
Do not rely on a prior year’s exam date, fee, or cut-off; these can change by institution and admission cycle.

sections.heading: Fees: institution-specific and time-sensitive
paragraphs: There is no single all-India M.Sc. Nursing tuition figure in the INC eligibility and syllabus materials; applicants should use the current fee notice of each institution and separately budget for hostel, mess, deposits, clinical materials, travel, and examination-related charges where applicable.

As dated public-sector context only, AIIMS’ 2025 M.Sc. prospectus listed tuition of ₹700 for the two-year M.Sc. Nursing course, plus separate registration, pot-money, caution-money, and other charges. That historical example should not be used as a current fee quote for AIIMS or any other college, because charges and inclusions can be revised.
bullets: Compare the full published fee schedule rather than tuition alone.
Confirm whether hostel and mess are available, compulsory, or charged separately.
Use the prospectus for the intended admission year as the authoritative source for costs.

sections.heading: Progression after M.Sc. Nursing
paragraphs: A major academic progression route is Ph.D. in Nursing. INC’s revised Ph.D. Nursing guidelines describe a part-time research-scholar pathway for candidates with M.Sc. Nursing or another Council-recognized postgraduate nursing degree, at least 60% in M.Sc. Nursing, and three years of teaching or clinical experience after the M.Sc.; the candidate must also be working in a relevant institution within the university’s jurisdiction. Selection may be on merit through an entrance route or M.Sc. Nursing marks, depending on the university’s process.

The M.Sc. curriculum’s explicit education, management, advanced-practice, specialty-clinical, and research components also provide relevant preparation for nursing education, clinical-specialist, management, research, and government-employment pathways. Actual eligibility for a Nursing Officer, Assistant Professor, Clinical Research Associate, or other role is determined by the recruiting employer, regulator, and current recruitment notice.
bullets: Ph.D. in Nursing: verify the university’s current eligibility, entrance, supervisor, and employment-jurisdiction conditions.
Teaching and faculty-track roles: check the relevant university, INC, UGC, and employer requirements rather than assuming the degree alone is sufficient.
Clinical, research, and government roles: match the chosen M.Sc. specialty with current employer recruitment criteria.
